observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to adhere with the documentary and reporting requirements of LBC 111 dated June 10, 2016 in order for them to identify and assess whether the existing LEEs are financially self-sufficient and profitable and provide them better decision making relative to the LEEs operations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Inventory and Disposal Committee and the General Services Officer to
immediately cause the disposal of these unserviceable properties to prevent its
further deterioration and for management to demand higher return if disposed of
thru sale or negotiation.
b. OIC-Accountant to make full disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements the
composition of the Property, Plant and Equipment which included the
unserviceable properties.
c. OIC-Accountant and the General Services Officer to consider the one-time
cleansing of its PPE accounts by observing the guidelines prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to update the status of the
Municipality’s PPE and to fairly present the account in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
1. Advise the OIC- Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to take
up the unidentified deposits by taking up the following suggested entry, viz:
Cash In Bank P28,548.76
Trust Liabilities P28,548.76
2. Require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to coordinate/communicate with the
Land Bank Officials and know the whereabouts of the deposits made.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant the following:
a. review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims,
ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents
and drop from the books those accounts that were not supported with necessary
documents.
b. revert payables aged more than two years to be without valid claims, pursuant to
Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended to Management to:
1.Direct the concerned end-user to submit justification on non-preparation and submission of Project Requirement, otherwise these expenditures will be considered as unnecessary that will be subsequently disallowed in audit.
2. Also, direct BAC to submit justification/explanation on processing the procurement of these items when these were not among the project requirement submitted by requisitioning Office.
We recommend that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements to verify the regularity and propriety of the procurement transactions prescribed by the guidelines during the state of public health emergency. Furthermore. Management is required to submit explanation on the regularity and propriety of the transactions totaling P15,519,999.00.

We recommended that Management to:
a. Execute an executive order to commence a one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment account and to establish the itemized listing of properties that should be recorded therein, and;
b. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the PPE accounts after complying to the disposal procedures provided under Section 267 of the Local Government Accounting Manual and Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b. Error in the accounting treatment of inventories with an aggregate amount of P10,371,429.00 affects the fair presentation of the financial statements. We recommended that Management strictly implement the perpetual inventory method in recording of supplies and materials and other items by coursing all purchases through the inventory account for each item purchased and recording all issuances therefrom as expenses or other asset account (e.g. CIP) using the moving average method of inventory costing, except however, in case of purchases made out of petty cash fund for immediate use which should be taken up as direct expenses. The ending balance of all supplies and materials inventory per books should then be reconciled with the physical count of such inventories.
c. Physical existence and validity of Inventories amounting to P9,331,051.39 cannot be ascertained due to non-preparation and submission of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). We recommend that the municipality conduct physical inventory count at least twice a year to establish the actual stock on hand as of the end of a given period and determine whether there were any losses or spoilage and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
1. Advise the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to closely monitor the development projects during the year as reflected in the AIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management to:
1. Direct the concerned end-user to submit justification on non-preparation
and submission of Project Requirement, otherwise these expenditures will
be considered as unnecessary that will subsequently disallowed in audit.
2. Also, direct BAC to submit justification/explanation on processing the

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary
requirements to verify the regularity and propriety of the procurement transactions
under the Bayanihan Act. Furthermore, Management is required to submit
explanation for the afore-cited deficiencies and courses of action to be taken to avoid
recurrence thereof.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the Operating Lease Receivable and Receipt from Market Operation accounts to reflect the true results of operation of the concerned economic enterprise and may assist in better management decisions.

We recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances of the Operating Lease Receivable and Receipt from Market Operation accounts to reflect the true results of operation of the concerned economic enterprise and may assist in better management decisions.

We recommended the  management to:

direct the offices concerned to develop strategies to expedite collections and to enforce loan payment per provisions of the MOA/Contract. 

exhaust all possible remedies to collect the Receivable accounts particularly those recipient who are still existing in the municipality; and

if the circumstances warrant, request for write-off for receivables where settlement/collectability can no longer be ascertained as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain financial and accounting records of special accounts under the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund, Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) and Economic Enterprises and,

Submit the related financial reports in order to facilitate immediate verification on the sources and uses of funds.

We recommended management to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) of the LGU  as set forth in COA Circular 2012-002 to properly provide management with accurate financial data on available resources of calamity fund and its utilization to facilitate monitoring on the status of the fund, to serve as guide in decision making and to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary and illegal disbursements of the fund.

We recommended the Management to: 

require the Municipal SWMB undertake the following courses of action:

Prepare and formulate the yearly plans and programs and provide appropriations from the Municipality’s annual budget to support the activities in the attainment of its targets as laid down in the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP;

Utilize immediately the acquired parcel of land for the operation and management of sanitary landfill in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 9003; and

Prioritize the funding for acquisition of additional garbage compactor so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

advise the SWM-TWG to monitor/enforce the strict implementation of the ESWM Plan specifically on the proper maintenance of MRFs in the barangays, schools and other offices.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to secure at all times Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing him to enter into contract with concerned contractors in behalf of the Municipality before contract perfection, for those projects under the lump-sum appropriation and fund transfers from various government agencies.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended the following courses of action:

Management must endeavor to implement disaster mitigation, preparedness and response capability programs and projects in accordance with its LDRRMF Investment Plan to effectively prevent the onslaught on lives and properties of the people of New Bataan in times of natural disaster.

Maximization of available resources on the local DRRMF for disaster mitigation and preparedness in order to build the resilience of the Municipality of New Bataan against disasters including climate change impacts.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to immediately address the causes of the delay and non-implementation in order to fast-track the implementation of development projects embodied under the Annual Investment Plan (AIP), in accordance with the time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the municipality; and

Advise the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to closely monitor the development projects during the year as reflected in the AIP.
observations.reason: Composition of Project Monitoring Committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the MEO and other concerned departments to closely monitor and ensure economic and efficient implementation of all projects under the pertinent National Programs by virtue of the fund transfers received from the National Government agencies for implementation.

We recommended the Management to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the yearly Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Team facilitate the review on municipality’s compliance with rules and regulations pertaining thereto, as well as to facilitate validation and evaluation of the projects, programs and activities implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended :


Establish/ and or prepare Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) and prepare Municipal CPC Annual Report.
observations.reason: Lacks Local Development Plan for Children due to data gathering constraints.

We recommended Management to:
require the Municipal SWMB undertake the following courses of action:
Prepare and formulate the yearly plans and programs and provide appropriations from the Municipality’s annual budget to support the activities in the attainment of its targets as laid down in the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP;

advise the SWM-TWG to monitor/enforce the strict implementation of the ESWM Plan specifically on the proper maintenance of MRFs in the barangays, schools and other offices.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended the following courses of action:
Management must endeavor to implement disaster mitigation, preparedness and response capability programs and projects in accordance with its LDRRMF Investment Plan to effectively prevent the onslaught on lives and properties of the people of New Bataan in times of natural disaster.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to immediately address the causes of the delay and non-implementation in order to fast-track the implementation of development projects embodied under the Annual Investment Plan (AIP), in accordance with the time frame to accelerate socio-economic growth and environmental development in the municipality; and
Advise the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to closely monitor the development projects during the year as reflected in the AIP.

We recommended Management to direct the MEO and other concerned departments to closely monitor and ensure economic and efficient implementation of all projects under the pertinent National Programs by virtue of the fund transfers received from the National Government agencies for implementation.

We recommended management to adhere with the documentary and reporting requirements of LBC 111 dated June 10, 2016 in order for them to identify and assess whether the existing LEEs are financially self-sufficient and profitable and provide them better decision making relative to the LEEs operations.

We recommended Management to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the yearly Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Team facilitate the review on municipality’s compliance with rules and regulations pertaining thereto, as well as to facilitate validation and evaluation of the projects, programs and activities implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended the following courses of action:

a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment to correct the reported balances of the affected PPE and Financial Asset accounts in the Financial Statements.

b. 	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy noted between the balances of Subsidy from Other Funds and Subsidy to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjusting entries. 

c. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the misclassified transactions to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended the management to:

a.	Require the Municipal  Accountant to fully recognize the RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of tax payers’ amount due and collectible for the year prepared by the Municipal Treasurer in order to record in the books not the mere income estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes and to fairly present the said accounts in the financial statements. 

b. 	Require the Municipal Treasurer identify the actual uncollected Real Property and Special Education Fund Taxes, excluding the penalties, to establish the balance of RPT/SET receivable as at yearend. Consequently, the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments to present fairly in the financial statements the balance of the said receivable accounts. 

c. 	Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly the accounting and treasury records to verify the accuracy and validity of their records and immediately provide corrective action for discrepancies noted.

d. 	Advice the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to consider purchasing a computer software to automate the treasury processes, enhance operational quality, ensure regulatory compliance, minimize human errors, and realize on-demand reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the required authority from the SB for the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract or incurred expense in behalf of the LGU to implement the projects totaling P3,422,963.49.

We also recommend that in the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budget, the Municipal Mayor should provide specific and sufficient details of the proposed projects/programs in compliance with COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended management to adhere strictly to the provisions Section 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular 2012-002 in the transfer of unexpended/unspent LDRRM funds to the special trust funds.

We recommended management to accomplish the lacking minimum standards on disaster preparedness, and mitigation as prescribed in Disaster Preparedness Plan 2011-2028 Volume 2, most especially on the category on service delivery, and the procurement of lacking equipment be considered in the LDRRM planning to fully comply with the minimum requirements.

We recommended management to ensure that appropriation ordinances in generic terms are supported with an authorization from the Local Sanggunian approving the itemized appropriation of these funds to avoid audit disallowance.

We recommended to the management to submit a written explanation why LDRRM Fund was disbursed for the expenditures mentioned above, in as much as the fund has its specific purpose as provided in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and

Further, refrain from charging expenses which are not among those prioritized to be charged against the LDRRM Fund as stated in the above circular.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant’s Office to strictly impose and deduct the liquidated damages from the claim of the suppliers/contractors.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

a. 	Review current manpower requirements of the Municipality vis-à-vis unimplemented PPAs. If the in-house capability is not adequate to implement PPAs as scheduled, consider the mode of implementation by contract for projects of substantial estimated project costs especially those where the target completion dates and/or year of implementation, have already lapsed; and

b. 	Create a monitoring team and closely follow through the periodic progress implementation of development projects to ensure their completion within the programmed timelines; and


c. 	Strengthen the conduct of proper planning and timely execution of PPAs as well as continuous monitoring and evaluation of PPAs to address issues that may be needed to be resolved during their implementation.

We recommended that the municipality’s development council headed by the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the optimum utilization of the Municipality’s 20% development fund. Likewise, we recommend that the management allocate properly the municipal budget to fully utilize the available resources to other important PPAs that are economically beneficial to its constituents.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended management to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s and continuing appropriations and prior years’ unexpended balance so that programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness program will be implemented within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to a sustained recovery in case of disaster/calamity will be strengthened to improve the disaster resilience of communities in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. 	Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor’s Office and devise a comprehensive strategy primarily focused on the collection of delinquent property taxes to reduce the outstanding amount of delinquencies substantially;

b. 	Request sufficient funding for the automation of the treasury process to enhance the quality of service and operation and plantilla position to facilitate effective property tax collection and assessment; and

c. 	Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer of the Municipality of Compostela to settle the duplication of assessment of RPUs and avoid the incurrence of additional delinquent accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal SWMB undertake the following courses of action:

a. 	Immediately utilize the acquired parcel of land for the operation and management of sanitary landfill in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 9003.

b. 	Prioritize the funding for the acquisition of additional garbage compactor so that the delivery of social services in terms of collection and disposal of waste would not be hampered.

Awards Committee to
justify and explain
the reasons on their
recommendation to
award the
procurement of labor
to a licensed regular
contractor in
violation of the
regulation as
mentioned above.
Henceforth,
Management should
comply strictly with
the regulation for a
similar transaction
that will be made in
the future.

We recommended that
the management
consider the
following courses of
action to improve
the efficiency of
project
implementation:
Direct the concerned
implementing offices
to address the
issues/ concerns that
caused the
non-implementation
and/or delay in the
completion of
projects;
Create a monitoring
team and closely
follow through the
periodic progress
implementation of
development
projects to ensure
their completion
within the programmed
timelines for the
attainment of the
planned socio-
economic
developmental goals
and provision of
desired benefits
without further delay
to intended
beneficiaries; and
Revisit the
guidelines on local
planning and
development, and
comply strictly with
the conditions for
the determination and
actual
implementation of
projects that
support the Annual
Investment Plan to
improve efficiency in
the local governance
as a whole.
observations.reason:
For the
P24,411,540.00-
worth project,
P21,638,530.00 were
completed.
The contractor of
Panag Upgrading of
Community Access
Road amounting to
P2,773,010.00
requested time
suspension for
variation order due
to unseen
unsuitable
materials.
The LGU is still on
the process of
creating the
monitoring team.
Revisit the
guidelines on local
planning and
development, and
comply strictly with
the conditions for
the determination
and actual
implementation of
projects that
support the Annual
Investment Plan to
improve efficiency
in the local
governance as a
whole.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the
following course of
action:
Intensify collection
through sending
follow-up
letters/notices of
delinquent stall/lot
rentals to remind
them of their
obligations.
Cause the immediate
Cancellation of the
business permit or
licenses in order not
to accumulate the
interest and
surcharges.
For those lessees
who are no longer in
business, confiscate
the stall/lots to
award to another
interested one.
observations.reason:
Out of P1,792,821.47,
a total of
P799,416.82 were
collected as of
December 31, 2018.
The Local Chief
Executive decided to
defer the
confiscation of the
stall/lot to give
ample time to the
delinquent stall/lot
leases to settle their
delinquencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the Municipality’s
Solid Waste
Management Board
find an efficient,
effective and
economical way to
utilize the sanitary
landfill as soon as
possible to avoid the
impending problem
for waste disposal.
We further
recommended that the
SWMB considers the
proposal of the
Municipal Engineer’s
Office (MEO) to
resolve the
inaccessibility issue
of the sanitary
landfill. It is also
recommended that the
management
appropriate enough
funds to realize the
MEO’s proposal of
opening a new road
for the immediate
utilization of the
sanitary landfill.
observations.reason:
Bad weather
conditions during
the construction
affect the
implementation of
the project in the
area.
Alternate proposed
road to SLF was not
materialized due to
some disapproval of
the affected
landowners.
An allotment for
concreting of some
critical road section
to SLF was approved
and for 2019
implementation.
DPWH is also
planning to
construct an
overflow box culvert
across MAA River to
SLF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the Municipal SWMB
fulfill the
requirements of the
EMB-DENR to fast-
track the turn-over of
the MRF for it to be
utilized immediately
and attain the
objectives of R.A.
9003 particularly
concerning proper
segregation,
composting and
recycling of wastes
in an
environmentally
sound manner.

We recommended that
the Municipal Mayor
direct the
responsible officials
of the Municipality
to make necessary
actions to facilitate
the implementation of
the projects/programs/activities so that the
targeted beneficiaries
may enjoy the
benefits to be
derived therefrom.
observations.reason:
Manurigao water
system was
completed.
For the Salintubig –
Cabinuangan, we are
on the process of
finding a new water
source, so that we
can proceed for the
variation order and
implementation.

observations.recommendation:

that the Municipal
Social Welfare and
Development Officer
and the Municipal
Agriculturist fast
track the

Sustainable
Livelihood Programs
to help the living
condition of the 4Ps
and “Typhoon Pablo”

make a follow-up
letter to require all
Non-Government

001 dated June 14,

observations.reason:
Still, a balance of
P1,415,818.55 exists
as at Dec. 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Grow Micro
Program - Technical
Working Group (GWP-
TWG) to establish a
loan policy indicating,
but not limited to,
the following
information: the loan
process; the
qualification and
assessment of the
borrowers based on
their capacity,

zation of payment

We recommended that the Local School Board: Consider and include only expenditures appropriately chargeable to Special Education Fund pursuant to RA 5447, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.2 01-A and 01-B during the planning and budget preparation. Refrain future payment of expenditures not allowed to be charged under the Special Education Fund to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances. For schools in dire need of establishing extension classes, submit request to the DepEd Division Superintendent supported with the computation of the number of pupils/students vis-à-vis number of regular DepEd teaching staff in the concerned elementary/secondary public schools in order to accommodate the salaries of teachers to be hired which can be appropriately charged against the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 4.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

    In view thereof, we recommended that Management:

    a. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary  adjustment
       on the omissions/errors to correct  the  reported  balances  of  the
       affected accounts in the Financial Statements;


    b. Prepare a reconciliation statement for the  discrepancies  noted  in
       the balances of Due to other NGAs account vis-a-vis NGAs records and
       effect the necessary adjusting entries, if  any  and  coordinate  or
       make representation to NGAs Accountant to settle the variance.;


    c.  Require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  compute   and   recognized
       depreciation of the PPE account.


    d. Observe the additional information required by  PPSAS  that  is  not
       shown on the face of the financial statements  but  relevant  to  an
       understanding.

It is recommended   that the Municipal  Social Welfare and  Development Officer  and the Municipal  Agriculturist fast  track the  implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Programs in order to help the living condition of the 4Ps and “Typhoon Pablo” affected families.
observations.reason: The balance of P1.6M was intended to purchase abaca but the supplier was not able to deliver the supply. This is subject to re-bidding.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), being the Municipal Development Council (MDC) Secretariat to clearly specify the programs/projects/ activities with its corresponding cost under the 20% Development Fund for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and the Annual Budget.
Require the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to coordinate with each other on infrastructure project details for the prompt preparation of the program of work and detailed estimates by the former.

We recommended that management strictly enforced Municipal Ordinance No.004, series of 2012 related to garbage fines and penalties in order to generate additional income for the municipality which can be used to fund program for Solid Waste management.

In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of perfected and awarded contracts of the LGU, we have recommended the following: Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of each semester and have it approved by the HOPE. Submit the duly approved PMR, furnishing a copy to Office of the Auditor, the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

Require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit the list of PPAs on its prescribed format every beginning of the year which shall include those at the planning stage and those on-going projects in pursuance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation of the projects.

Management should make a follow-up letter to require all Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations with unliquidated fund transfers and settle and/or liquidate their respective accountability, pursuant to COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The Management requested to the Sangguniang Bayan a resolution for an allowance for bad debts but the SB returned it for further verification/ inspection as to the existence of the cooperatives. Likewise, the MTO send collection letters to the different NGOs and partial payment was made and promised to pay.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the 50% share of the Special Education Tax to the provincial government to aid the latter in the funding and implementation of priority programs and projects under the Special Education Fund.

Require the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a government Official Receipts (OR)/ Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established. PCL collections should be deposited as trust fund and should be disbursed in accordance with the board of director’s resolutions, subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations. Ensure that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a government official receipt which is Accountable Form 51 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances. Require the SB member, Hon. Justino D. Vargas, to submit the deficient documentary requirements as presented in this AOM to the auditors’ office.

Direct the Grow Micro Program - Technical Working Group (GWP-TWG) to establish a loan policy indicating, but not limited to, the following information: the loan process; the qualification and assessment of the borrowers based on their capacity, character, condition, co-maker; the required documents for loan application; the loan term; computation of interest and penalty, if any; frequency of collection; prioritization of payment whether penalty and interest first before the allocation of payment to principal; and other information that might be useful in the operation of this program and for future loan programs.
observations.reason: The Management requested to the Sangguniang Bayan a resolution for an allowance for bad debts but the SB returned it for further verification/ inspection as to the existence of the cooperatives. Likewise, the MTO send collection letters to the different NGOs and partial payment was made and some promised to pay.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      It is recommended that the Municipal Social  Welfare  and  Development
Officer and the Municipal Agriculturist fast  track  the  implementation  of
the Sustainable Livelihood Programs in order to help  the  living  condition
of the 4Ps and “Typhoon Pablo” affected families.

Recommendations:

      Require the Municipal Planning  and  Development  Coordinator  (MPDC), being  the  Municipal  Development  Council  (MDC)  Secretariat  to  clearly
specify the programs/projects/activities with its corresponding cost   under
the 20% Development Fund   for inclusion in the Annual Investment  Plan  and
the Annual Budget.

      Require the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to coordinate with each  other
on infrastructure project details for the prompt preparation of the  program
of work and detailed estimates by the former.

      Direct the Municipal Engineer to  strictly  administer  and  supervise
the  implementation  of  development  projects  and  likewise   advise   the
Municipal Planning and  Development  Coordinator  to  strictly  monitor  its
implementation.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management  strictly  enforced  Municipal  Ordinance
No.004, series of 2012 related to garbage fines and penalties  in  order  to
generate additional income for the municipality which can be  used  to  fund
program for Solid Waste management.

Recommendations:

      In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the  procurement
process and implementation of perfected and awarded contracts  of  the  LGU,
we have recommended the following:
     1. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to  prepare  the  Procurement
        Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of
        each semester and have it approved by the HOPE.

     2. Submit the duly approved PMR, furnishing a copy to  Office  of  the
        Auditor, the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in  printed
        and electronic format within fourteen ((14) calendar days after the
        end of each semester.

Recommendation:

      Require the Municipal  Engineer  and  other  concerned  officials  to
submit the list of PPAs on its prescribed  format  every beginning of the
year which shall include those at the  planning  stage  and  those  on-going
projects in pursuance to the provisions of COA Circular No.  2013-004  dated
January 30, 2013 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation  of
the projects.

Recommendation:


      Management  should  make  a  follow-up  letter  to  require  all  Non-
Government  Organizations/People’s  Organizations  with  unliquidated   fund
transfers and  settle  and/or  liquidate  their  respective  accountability,
pursuant to COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007  and  COA  Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Recommendations:

      We recommend that the Local School Board:

  1. Consider and include  only  expenditures  appropriately  chargeable  to
     Special Education Fund pursuant to RA 5447, RA 7160  and  DECS-DBM-DILG
     Joint Circular No.2 01-A  and  01-B  during  the  planning  and  budget
     preparation.


  2. Refrain future payment of expenditures not allowed to be charged  under
     the Special Education Fund to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.


  3. For schools in dire need  of  establishing  extension  classes,  submit
     request  to  the  DepEd  Division  Superintendent  supported  with  the
     computation of  the  number  of  pupils/students  vis-à-vis  number  of
     regular DepEd teaching  staff  in  the  concerned  elementary/secondary
     public schools in order to accommodate the salaries of teachers  to  be
     hired which can be appropriately charged against the Special  Education
     Fund.

Program funds downloaded by national government agencies be carefully evaluated before implementation to ensure its effectiveness.  After careful evaluation, if programs are not viable, make representation s to the donor agency for the realignment of program funds to other identified priority programs/projects of the municipality to avoid wastage of funds and to be able to attain the pro-poor objectives of the program.

Management should require all Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations with unliquidated fund transfers to immediately settle and/or liquidate their respective accountability, pursuant to COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant disclosed that they sent collection/ demand letters to the beneficiar ies but there was no reply.
observations.reason: Despite of demand letters send to the NGOs/POs no liquidat ion was received by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: Requisition and acquisition of fuel shall be made on a semi-monthly/monthly estimated fuel consumption of the various heavy equipment and motor vehicles of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had stopped procuring fuel, oil and lubricants in large quantities/volume.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineering Office to prepare on a monthly basis and per heavy equipment, the Heavy Equipment Utilization Report and furnished the same the Office of the COA Auditor in order to have immediate verification on the sources and uses of this fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant particularly the motorpool has been regularly submitting fuel consumption report for heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a government Official Receipts (OR)/Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established.
PCL collections should be deposited as trust fund and should be disbursed in accordance with the board of director’s resolutions, subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
Ensure that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a government official receipt which is Accountable Form 51 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
Require the SB member, Hon. Justino D. Vargas, to submit the deficient documentary requirements as presented in this AOM to the auditors’ office. Failure to do so may result to audit suspension/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required lacking documents of Justino Vargas
observations.reason: The management had complied the supporting documents of DV’s cited.  However, for the registra tion fee of PCL seminars/ training they still used the personalized receipt.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a subsidiary ledger account for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) in order to facilitate immediate verification on the sources and uses of this fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already created a separate subsidiary ledger for MDRRMF both General Fund and Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the preparation and submission of the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA every end of each month pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: .The Municipal Accounting Office has regularly submitted their MDRRMF utilization report.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to strictly comply with the preparation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and its corresponding LDRRMF Investment Plan annually pursuant to Item C Section 12 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already submitted their LDRRMF Plan and LDRRMF Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation: Require the Bids and Awards Committee to enforce the posting of Performance Security as required under Section 39 of the IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has already invited 2 observers in all stages of procuremen t.

observations.recommendation: Require the Bids and Awards Committee to enforce the posting of Performance Security as required under Section 39 of the IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already required the winning bidder to post Performanc e Security.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended to require the GSO to submit approved Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review and evaluation of the various contracts entered into by the Municipality. Also, see to it that Purchase Orders and other documents in the procurement process are duly accomplished, dated and received by concerned parties in compliance with the provisions of RA 9184.
Further, require the BAC to submit copy of resolutions for the procurement of meals and snacks that indicates the basis for the determination of the winning bidder.
observations.managementAction: The approved PO together with other supporting documents were regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
Immediately use the purchased lot for dumping site for the operation and management of waste disposal to sustain the effective implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the local government.
Stop the operation of the present disposal site and strictly implement the waste segregation policy of the Municipality. Restrict also the entry of unauthorize d persons picking wastes at the disposal area in order to prevent the scattering of wastes, which is detrimental to the environment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already used the purchased lot for dumping site for waste disposal.

observations.recommendation:
The management through the GAD Focal Person shall seek approval of its GAD Plan and Budget and secure Certificate of Approval from the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Regional office for the proper integration of the GAD Programs, Projects and activities in its Annual Investment Plan.
Likewise, the Municipal Planning and Development Office should establish a Gender database in order to have reliable and sufficient information for gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulations.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had seek approval of GAD Plan and Budget from DILG.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the CY 2015 Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Auditor to be able to validate and evaluate the implemented programs,projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had submitted their GAD Accomplishme nt Report.

observations.recommendation: Direct   the  Grow Micro  Program - Technical Working Group (GWP-TWG) to establish a loan policy indicating, but not limited to, the following information: the loan process; the qualification and assessment of the borrowers based on their capacity, character, condition, co-maker; the required documents for loan application; the loan term; computation of interest and penalty, if any; frequency of collection; prioritization of payment whether penalty and interest first before the allocation of payment to principal; and other information that might be useful in the operation of this program and for future loan programs.
